QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_31

question.text: A company with annual net sales of Rs.1 crore, 10 lakh outstanding shares, and CMP of Rs.40 has a P/S ratio of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Annual Net Sales per Share = 1,00,00,000 / 10,00,000 = Rs.10. P/S = CMP / Sales per share = 40 / 10 = 4. **The Trap:** Common error: dividing CMP by total sales (Rs.40/Rs.1 crore) instead of per-share sales. Always calculate per-share values first. **Cross-Exam Context:** P/S ratio calculation is tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 3. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** P/S of 4 means the market values each rupee of sales at Rs.4. Compare with industry peers to assess relative valuation.
